source: iConnections Global Alts 2026 — AI, Tesla, defense, energy — what comes next
summary: xAI has the lowest cost-per-token due to superior data center construction and GPU utilization
summary_extended: Baker argues that xAI has achieved the lowest cost-per-token among AI model providers due to structural advantages in data center construction and GPU utilization. Specifically, he claims xAI builds data centers faster and at lower cost than any peer, and runs its GPUs at unusually high utilization rates, creating a durable cost advantage. This positions xAI as a potentially dominant AI model provider if cost-per-token is the key competitive variable in the industry. However, xAI is a private company with no US-listed equity, so there is no directly investable public vehicle tied to this thesis. Baker does not name any public company as a beneficiary or competitor in this specific claim, making the investment implication limited to monitoring xAI's competitive pressure on public AI model providers.
detail: Baker claims xAI builds data centers faster and at lower cost than peers, runs GPUs at higher utilization, and therefore has the lowest cost per token in the industry. This is a structural cost advantage.
confidence_evidence: Stated as a factual claim but sourced from Baker's own analysis; no third-party corroboration cited
quote_fragment: Xxii has the lowest cost per token because they build data centers in a superhuman way
